174: Aesthetic novel set in Victorian London; references to Greek mythology, Renaissance art, and London geography.
526: Novella set on the Thames and the Congo; references to British imperial history, Roman colonization, and exploration.
768: Gothic novel set on the Yorkshire moors; archaic dialect, references to the English countryside and social class.
1260: Bildungsroman set in early 19th-century England; references to natural history, fairy tales, and the Bible.
1342: Social satire of Regency-era England; references to landed gentry, country estates, and period manners.
1399: Realist novel of Russian high society; references to Moscow and St. Petersburg social life, European culture, and Orthodox Christianity.
1400: Bildungsroman set in early Victorian England; references to the Kent marshes, London, convict transportation, and social class.
1524: Early modern English verse drama; archaic vocabulary, stage directions mixed with dialogue, references to Danish and Norwegian history.
2554: Psychological novel set in St. Petersburg; references to Russian urban poverty, pawnbrokers, and moral philosophy.
2701: Dense philosophical prose with biblical, classical, and natural-history allusions; references to whaling cultures, world religions, and Romantic poetry.
3600: Renaissance essay dense with Latin quotations, classical philosophy, and references to ancient Greek and Roman thinkers.
4300: Densely allusive modernist prose; references include Homer's Odyssey, Catholic liturgy (Latin Mass), Greek literature, Shakespeare, and Irish history.
4363: Philosophical treatise; references to German idealism, ancient Greek philosophy, and European intellectual history.
8387: Psychological novel set in 1890s Christiania (Oslo); references to Norwegian streets, landmarks, newspapers, and European philosophy.
